idea.hook: Ever wondered what a teaspoon of neutron star weighs? 🤯
facts: A neutron star forms from a collapsed massive star, packing immense mass into a tiny space.
It can have 1.4 times the mass of our Sun (2.8 x 10^30 kg), yet be only ~20-25 km wide.
This makes it one of the densest objects in the universe, second only to black holes.
idea.payoff: A single TEASPOON of neutron star material would weigh over 5 billion tons – more than all 8 billion humans on Earth combined!
idea.title: A Teaspoon Weighs WHAT?! 🤯💫

scenes.text: Ever wondered what a TEASPOON of a neutron star weighs? 🤯
scenes.visual: Dynamic, swirling space nebula background. The text appears centrally, with a subtle glow around the exclamation point.
Animation of a large, bright star (supergiant) rapidly collapsing inwards, shrinking to a tiny, incredibly dense glowing point. Nebula background continues.

Split screen: Left shows a large, yellow Sun graphic. Right shows a minuscule, glowing neutron star graphic with a label 'Diameter: ~20-25 km'. An arrow points from Sun to neutron star with '1.4x Mass'.

scenes.text: One of the DENSEST objects in the universe!
scenes.visual: An infographic scale comparing densities: 'Regular Matter' -> 'White Dwarf' -> 'Neutron Star'. The 'Neutron Star' segment glows intensely, emphasizing its extreme density. The text is bold and impactful.

scenes.text: A single TEASPOON: over 5 BILLION TONS! More than all 8 BILLION HUMANS combined!
scenes.visual: A stylized graphic of a teaspoon fills with a tiny, intensely glowing speck. Next to it, an Earth icon with small human silhouettes, and a giant '5 BILLION TONS' text overlay, showing the comparison visually.
